text: After a couple uses little holes started in several areas. Cover is not very soft.

"Super heavy duty"???  What product did that reviewer buy?  These things are cheap, cheap, cheap, thin, thin, thin, small, small, small.... but.......................... at a buck and some cents a hanger, what did I expect realistically?  Not returning, not worth it.  Yes you can close your door with these things.  But understand these things are small.  You need to be a tall person to reach the top of the door to put your towel on it, and don't make it a big towel okay?

Set of 3 trays. They are white with little dots of silicone to create the non slip surface. They are graduated in size, two have handles at the ends, the smallest tray does not have handle, but is lipped.  All of the trays have lipped, curved edges.  I like them.  What you put on them will not slip around!  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

I make my own bone broth and this one cup version is perfect.  The frozen soup pops right out, one cup at a time, or all - you choose.  The lids help keep the frame solid when you set it in the freezer.  I use it freeze casseroles - soups - stews... I am considering purchasing the 1/2 cup version for gravies ? and other things you want smaller portions of.  You get 2 sets (4 pieces, two cubes, two lids).  The cubes are an aqua color, the lids are whitish- not quite completely clear but you can see through them.  They wash up in the dishwasher perfectly.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

Nice looking  11 inch ceramic pan, with the flared out sides, no lid. The outside is a Candy Apple Red, the silicone handle is red. Can be used on a Induction stove, and electric. The inside is slick, and smooth. Pan is PTFE and PFOA free. Not a heavy pan.  It says, you can put in the dishwasher, but if you want this pan to last awhile, Do Not put this pan in the dishwasher, and your good knifes. Do not cook on high heat also.  The surface is smooth, I still used a little bit of Avocado oil when making veggies with eggs over them. I cook on a gas stove, and this pan seems to cook ok. Although underneath the pan just using it one time, where the pan sits on my stove  grates some of the red has come Off!  I have Allot of different types of cookware, from expensive, to very inexpensive, and also some over over 60 years old, and I have Never had any type of Colored, or stainless cookware scratch off or anything.  I personally do not think this cookware will last over 2 yrs if used Everyday. I would rate This Allure cookware as something that is low grade, will not last long, and would sell cheap at Wallmart or Target.

I purchased these to put little brownie squares on... but the options are limitless.  They are white plastic... sturdy and the perfect size for a bite.  I have even hand washed a few I used with my grandsons and reused.  I don't they are meant to be... but I like to reuse as much as I can.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

Quality made, attractive salt and pepper shaker. These make a great addition to any household and would make a terrific bridal shower or housewarming gift (or just a gift for yourself). I use these for more casual settings (although you could use them for everything) and I have received a load of compliments from family and friends on them since I started placing them on my table. On top of looking good, they perform excellently, grinding my peppercorns to perfection. I highly recommend them these to everyone who takes pride in how their table is set.
Very good looking. My wife liked it!
Only 90 Days old and it displays RED. Replaced Filter (that was purchased at time of purchase), and still displays RED. Home is a tiny (500 sq feet) and brand new home as well.
Piece of junk!  Zucchini gets smooshed up and the spirals don't come apart into strands. Don't waste your $ or time with this.

Was a little disappointed that it wasn't very thick - but it is pretty and soft.  I purchased a prize for family christmas bingo.  Time will tell! Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful!
Disappointed.  Very expensive for such a small selection of shells and most not big enough to show through the thick layer of sand.
Nothing special.

Followed directions to the letter, potatoes did not cook. Added more time... Potatoes (2) did not cook. Still more time... Nada. Tried this three different times with normal size potatoes, never cooked. Put potatoes on plate in microwave, used potato setting and had perfect potatoes! What a waste of time and money for me.
Very disappointed.  It came with a crack and piece missing

I purchased a weird corelle pattern that doesn't come with lunch size plates and so... I purchased the white ones.  These will work.  They are plain white and will work just fine.  Arrived whole, no chips or cracks.  Hooray Corelle! Light weight and yet TOUGH.  Love them.

These mats are THIN, and nowhere near grey!! You can see them in the foreground, with the fabulous grey  mats I had bought from Wellhouse May in the background. They do not match even slightly - these are more like a cream! I am so very disappointed as I'd wanted to set my table - silicone mats are great for everyday spills. I cannot return them easily as I live in Jamaica. I would never ever order these mats again. This is why accuracy in discriptions is so important! Back to square one🙈

even per the instructions, you have to let them sit on the counter a bit (however long) and then you have to break out each cube. You aren't supposed to twist them at all, which means one. cube. at. a time. It's annoying for an ice tray. The trays I bought at the dollar store are quick, easy and done. Double walled insulated cups - freeze if you want cold - I think they work best with cold  - but you can put hot drinks in.  I hand wash.  They are plastic with a silicone strip for holding.  Having said this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.
Nice bento that is huge and has a lot of compartments
Really?  A this metal tube with no bottom for $12.?  I’m the foolish one for buying it but I needed the shape/fit.  Very cheaply made.

Love the size of this, actually made for a 13 gallon bag.  Major flaw, however, is in the design, it's nearly impossible to get the bag to attach as they instruct!  Spent 10 minutes fighting with it, and then just left it halfway in the can and put something on the top to make the lid stay shut.  Absolutely useless as a kitchen trash can, it will now live in the garage without the lid for excess recycle items.  Total waste of money.

These VTOPMART FOOD STORAGE CONTAINERS (6Pack, 1.5L) were disappointing.  They are slightly too short to hold a cardboard carton of one dozen large eggs.  If you use the lids, they don’t hold very much.  The containers themselves take up a lot of refrigerator space.  The drainage liners are flimsy.  The containers are NOT dishwasher safe.  They are fairly expensive for what they are (about $18 on Amazon, or $3 each).  They seem like they belong in a Dollar Store for about $1 each, because they’re undersized and not very useful.

Love the bright colors and sea theme, I just wish that the soft side was of higher quality, but I guess for the price, what can I expect. It says on the packaging to wash it to soften up blanket, so I will try that.

Just what I needed!  My shoes were all over the floor so I came to Amazon for an over the door shoe holder.  This was a perfect solution.  The mesh holders are wonderful because my shoes can breath after Im done wearing them and it fits right over the door, easy assembly.  Each pocket fits a shoe.  I love it!

[[VIDEOID:71f3ca72ac6e53b145a8a3a9b6b43d71]] Sometimes I receive a product that is so amazing and so innovative that I end up buying it again and again. Other times I get products that I do not like but cannot find fault with them beyond preferences. Still other times I find myself with a product that just doesn’t work well. Unfortunately, this is one of those products that seems like a neat idea but doesn’t fit the need very well.  I will start by saying that I received one defective unit in the twin pack. That’s all right – I am not basing my review on the defective unit as it is an exception. I speak about this in my video and I show the problem. But – please realize that the defect is the exception, not the rule, and I am reviewing the non-defective unit.  The concept is simple – put coffee in the basket, seal it up and drop it in hot water and steep like you’re making tea. This is a good concept and in theory would work well, except that the sliding lid leaves a gap so that some of the grounds escape into the water, leaving some grit in the bottom of the cup. This could have been remedied with a small gasket between the basket and lid.  I will confess to being disappointed in this product. It does not work well for me and I cannot in good conscience recommend it for coffee drinkers. It is a better fit for tea, but it is marketed for coffee specifically.  Disclosure: I was provided a sample of this product in exchange for a fair, honest and thorough review. Even though I received this product at minimal or no cost I have rated and reviewed this product without bias, based solely on my experience with it.

This is a lovely, but sturdy little lotus flower that will burn the skinny incense sticks.  Because the flower is so sturdy, it is easy to clean.  It is also striking in a the black ceramic plate.  The lotus is a shiny glaze.  The black plate is a satin finish.  About 4" in diameter, including the black base.

Update--I have been using this toaster for maybe two weeks, and its failing fast! It went from a 4 atar to a One star, could be even less! It is now only getting the bread toasted or burned on the top and bottom of the Bread. Does Not toast in the middle of the bread, although it looks like some if the coils are on and Orange! I usually toast a Full Grain Organic Bread, and sometimes Sour Dough, and raison. I have never seen this happen with any toaster before. Not even with a inexpensive Black & Decker toaster oven, That  acually Toasted very even. When I now push in the crumb drawer on the side It's not coming out far enough to grab it, and clean it.  Needless to say, I'm very disappointed with this toaster not to work after two weeks. The toaster looks good, but the Quality is not there. A waste of money.   -------------------------------- Good looking moderen style 2 slice toaster. There is a button to press on the front bottom for frozen bread that works good. A button to press for bagels, that will get the Inside cut part toasty, and the round out side of the bagel is not crisp. If you want to check your toast just lift up the push botton on the right side, and you can see if its too dark ,or light for The type of bread your toasting. When I just want the toast , This Russel Hobbs toasts pretty even toast on both sides. When you press down to toast, the inside closes in on your toast so it does Not  fall to one side and burn your toast, A good feature. This toaster is So light weight when I went  to moveit back under the cabinet it fell over! I can put 1 long piece of sliced french bread in. On the side is the very narrow shallow crumb tray. I don't have a problem with finger prints. I just use a slightly damp dish towel and wipe it down.  I thought this was going to take up allot less space then my heavy English chrome Rowlett toaster ( like the Dualit) measures 15 in wide x 7 in. Deep, But its also a 4 slice toaster. This Russell Hobbs measures 15 in. Wide x 5 1/4 in Deep.2 slice toaster. Not that much of a differents, and we realize we need a 4 toaster as we have allot of company and family. Also the Hobbs is not any faster to toast with. I'm not sure it is glass on the front!! I think its some type of plastic, but it looks good.  I'm giving this a 4 star for my toast coming out even. The modern look is sleek , and clean looking. But This is so light weight it seems like a flimsy  toaster, and I think its over price. There is also a bread  , croissant  warmer for the top that I have not used yet, and not sure I will!.

I came across this butter knife and had to purchase it.  Trying to butter certain items is so irritating to me.  A knife that separates butter to make it spread easier?  Yes!  Works as stated.  Makes buttering much easier.  Nice design.  Even my husband liked it.  Washes up nicely in the dishwasher.

With this USA HEAVY DUTY NONSTICK HALF SHEET BAKING PAN (1051HS-1), the emphasis is certainly on “heavy”--the empty pan weighs almost 4 pounds!  If you use this pan regularly, you'll have terrific biceps.  I’m at a loss to explain why anyone would need such a weighty sheet pan, given that sheet pans are used for baking cookies and/or roasting vegetables.  In my experience, the food comes out okay even if a sheet pan warps with high oven heat.  The pan is made of “aluminized steel”.  It has a nice nonstick finish, but must be hand-washed.  (The care instructions are clear:  “Do not place in dishwasher.”)  Oven temperatures should NOT exceed 450 degrees F., which is standard for silicone-coated bakeware.  The care instructions also recommend using an aluminum foil liner or a silicone baking mat “when roasting vegetables or baking higher fat foods such as french fries or meat”.  In my household, any energy savings gained from the use of this heavyweight baking sheet will be erased by the cost of aluminum foil linings and the water used in hand-washing.
Works perfect in the fridge. Very sturdy, durable, easy to keep clean.
was a gift she loved it but i bought one for me when i saw how cute it was i ordered a 2nd  i dont put up a tree  but i just snipped the gold thread and put him with my other mini animals  love it !!
It looks nice on picture, but when I got it I realized that quality is worse then I thought. Button part was partly broken and all in all experience was upsetting.

I had to re read the description on this for proof..it is described as a fragrant oil..It smells like a very strong alcohol scent..nothing like the woodsy scent at all. First of all , getting the little plastic stopper out of the bottle was extremlly hard !!! I tried using a screw driver to get under the lip to pry it out..no such luck..after many many tries it finally budged and then I was able to get it out. Then the scent hit my nostrils..it was so strong it burned the inside of my nose. I dont see how this disgusting product got so many good reviews. I do have one good thing to say about this. I like the design of the bottle, and how the cap goes on and holds the reeds. Im going to use it after I dump out the contents and wash it out a few times. Take this review as you want.
beautiful addition to my shell decor.

This is one of those incense burners that burns both the skinny stick (the mud man's fishing pole) and then there is a position with a hole at the top of the ceramic piece that holds one back burning incense cone.  They included both the cones and the sticks.  I don't care for the burning cone smell - it just smells smoky.  And not in a good way.  The stick incense was similar but less offensive (my personal opinion).  I was disappointed that the whole unit is painted very dark. So, unless you down light it, you can't really see the detail.  It's not fine art, but it's cute.  It's just painted really dark.  It's shiny glazed ceramic.  Except for the mud man - he is matte.  I made an additional purchase of some tiny fairy garden gravel to put into the bottom of the "pond" for ease of mind and the ash of the incense - they are like little glittery sparkle rocks in the pond.

Everything but the boiled eggs is rubbery and gross. If you don't have space to dedicate to a burner, this is useful as it doesn't have a large footprint. But DON'T use it to make anything other than boiled eggs. You will be soooo disappointed.

Unfortunately I have to give this wine rack a poor review. It looks like it's a nice wine rack, made of solid wood with beautiful finish.  However, in the box was the wrong number of parts. I got one too many 2-dowel arms and one too few 1-dowel arms. The result is a wine rack than cannot be assembled.  Mistakes do happen. I am trying to contact the manufacturer to see if I can get the right part shipped so I can properly assemble the rack. Assuming I can get the right piece from them, I will rewrite this review, factoring in the customer service experience as well. So far I am having a very difficult time tracking down contact information for the manufacturer as there is no contact info in the package and I have not had success finding company information online.  Until I can reach the manufacturer and try for resolution, I have a useless box of parts and I cannot give this wine rack a favorable review.

These stools (we purchased the cherry color) are definitely worth the effort of assembling them! My husband, who's the pro in our house at putting together our impulse-buy Ikea finds, assembled both of these stools in about 1.5 hours. He said some of the legs were not properly labeled according to the instruction manual, and screwing the cross-pieces onto the legs was somewhat awkward. I don't think I would have had the patience for it, haha. However, once assembled (see pics) the stools were perfect! The cushions are comfortable, and the cross-pieces on the stool legs are the perfect place to rest your feet. All four legs of each stool sit flush to the floor with no rocking or unsteadiness. One of the reasons I liked these stools over other options is because there are no buttons or other studs that could be broken, pulled, or chewed off. Ha! I almost took off a star for the assembly process, but in the end, they're such great, solid stools that I'm sticking with 5 stars.
Not a good product

I bought these because the dimensions were perfect for the holiday bread loaves I was making and giving as gifts.  They are plastic, however, nicer than a paper plate... I would have preferred  glass, but nothing fit better than these.  I purchased some knives and included some special packets of apple cider.  Wrapped all in cellophane and raphia ribbon. This are just what I needed (a tiny heart cutter)- and it has one) and lots of other geometric shapes.  Comes is a round metal container, however, once you open it, you will never get the lid back on - so whip out the rubber bands.  The cutters are thin metal, sharp on end, rounded metal on the other.  I'm sure they'll last a long time as I rarely use cutters, but would be fun for charcuterie or other things you might need small cutters of --- each shape comes with 3 sizes.  Am a little disappointed the metal holder is sort of junk - but you get what you pay for.  If I can find another container that will hold them all neatly, I will move them and toss the container.

This is a mini set... so don't expect a full size one.  But that is exactly what I wanted.  I plan to use it for hot chocolate with my grandsons 5 and 8.  So the sizing is perfect for me.  I also plan to use it for small tea parties.  It is a bone white. One of the cups has a slight flaw on the lip of the cup - I'm holding it in the pic... but it's smooth and doesn't bother me.  Comes just as pictured, 4 cups, one teapot, one lid and one bamboo tray.  The box calls it a coffee set... perhaps espresso would work.  I washed it, top rack, in my dishwasher, no problems.  To be honest, it's perfect for me.  And it may be small in size, but as the pictures I have included show, the set is actually a decent size - although smaller than some.  The handles on the cups are nice when what you are drinking is hot.  I plan to use it happily for years, however, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

I was looking for something to spruce up my living room. I previously had had some standard gold rods which really had seen better days along with cloth tie backs. I saw Levelor Sienna rods and holdbacks and decided to try them out. First the quality of them is amazing. Second, the color is dark and rich and looks so much nicer than the old gold ones. They make my living room look rich and have transformed the room from a standard looking room into a beautiful designer room. Thanks Levelor and Amazon. These are just what I needed!
Replacements for my existing set.

I purchased some very expensive pillow shams for my 16x16 pillows.  My pillows couldn't be stuffed into the shams because they were too fat - the opening on the shams was horribly too small.  So I purchased these in hopes of being able to fold them and get them into the expensive shams.  Worked perfectly.  I will say they are not super fluffy... so I put another fluffier pillow (also they were smaller 12x12)  to add a little more body because the expensive shams were less than perfect... and that worked.  These pillows arrived sucked down in plastic, but once released they fluffed up nicely.  They have a white zippered cover on them.  While they are not super fluffy, they would not have been able to get into the shams if they were - besides these pillows are really just for looks so it all works for me.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope my review was helpful to you!

When my two 21 year old ceramic heaters started showing signs of failing I started hunting for new ones.  After a long and arduous search I decided to give this one a try.  I am very glad I did.  We have a living room that is about 400 sq ft. that opens to both a sun room and the kitchen, about 900 sq ft total.  This heater was able to keep the entire area in the upper 60s when it was below freezing outside, no sunshine.  It took 1 hour to heat this area from 58 to 68.  I do believe if this was utilized in a closed area of 400 to 500 sq ft., it would be more than adequate.  The construction is pure and simple.  It uses a small blower similar to what is used in your furnace to distribute the heat.  It is much quieter than fan driven heaters, has a higher cfm (cubic feet per minute) output and the delivery of the heat is very warm, and "softer" than fan driven models.  It tends to make a sizable area around it feel cozy as best as I can describe it.  When plugged in and turned on high it doesn't seem to have any more loading on the circuit than a standard hair dryer.  It is much more efficient in producing and distributing 1500W worth of heating than anything I've ever seen.  The unit does remain cool to the touch except one small area above the heater core on top where you might describe it as barely warm.  The filter on the back is basically a hair screen, not a dust filter.  The power cord is 6 foot long, adequately sized, with a standard 3 prong outlet connection.  The casters are adequate for hard flooring, not carpet.  But the unit itself does not feel like it is over 20 lbs. to me.  The remote is cute.  I'll be curious to see the electric bill.  We are now successfully using this one 1500W heater to warm the same area it required two 1500W ceramics to heat previously.  My only last concern would be with longevity.  With a 3 year warranty I expect with normal care and maintenance (cleanings) this heater should be around a long time.  I will update if I run into any problems.  UPDATE 2-17-2014:  This unit is now failing.  It still produces heat on the Lo setting, but produces much less on the Hi side then when new.  Now the high and low side are producing an equal amount of heat.  The unit has been subjected to no rough handling whatsoever.  The filter has been cleaned exactly as recommended.  I can only conclude that something inside the unit has failed.  Considering this unit has a 3 year warranty and has displayed a failure within 4 months is quite irritating.  I will be inquiring how much this would cost me to ship it back to the manufacturer for repairs, but no matter what the cost I find failure within this short of a time span pathetic, thus I have revised my rating on this product.  With this kind of performance I could NOT RECOMMEND purchasing this product based on longevity.
Says it is 42by 84. It was only 23.5 on the top with the pleats and 51 across the bottom.

I bought 2 of these frames.  The cardboard that is supposed to protect the corners scuffed up both frames.  All 4 corners were ruined.  I had to buy some Krylon and repaint them.  The little circle on the frame in the picture is a sticker they put right on the front of the frame.  It left adhesive residue that I had to use cleaners to remove.  On the back of the frames there are sawtooth corners to hang the frame.  This means you'll have to put 2 holes in the wall to hang each frame.  Seems like a bit much since the frame is not very heavy. I am very disappointed in this frame.
These are so pretty....thank you so much for replacing the broken one with a whole new set and with no hassle MERRY CHRISTMAS

So... if you make deviled eggs to take any where... you NEED these.  The trays come out... stuff your eggs, set on the tray.  Then place the tray in the box (easy because of the finger holes conveniently placed on the ends - no fuss no mess).  Then snap on the lid and VIOLE!  No egg smell in the fridge.  Keeps nicely for home use or if your taking somewhere, a way to keep your eggs from being destroyed by the plastic wrap on top.  You can remove them from the boxes and put them on a decorative plate when you arrive.  You can also stack more than one set together. Daughter stole mine.  Ha.  So, I got more.  I highly recommend going for the set of 2.  I wash mine in the top rack of dishwasher, noooo problem.  These really take the yucky/messy parts about deviled eggs away!  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.
Cheaply made.  Had a lot of jars that did not seal canned for 45 years. Stick with Kerr or ball. Not China crap.

I measured my club chair to make sure this cover would fit!   I ordered the gray, I like the color.  It’s not dark, or too light. It’s also kind of shiny. It’s a little tricky putting this cover on, so look at the directions. The big thick elastic part goes under and behind the cushion.  We are having a hard time getting this to fit smooth, and to stay Tuck in , in the back of the cushion, and the rollers I can see, and it doesn’t look good, or we are doing something wrong ! I have to say my Cushion lifts up in the back, but does not come Off the chair, that could be my problem , although would Like to hear if that was anyone else’s?  The way the cover fits now is useless to me. 2 star.

Nice looking indoor,  outdoor Hamilton beach  grill... Thats about all it is. Is a gimmick, and looks cute. Cooks enough for two people, or 3 !  There is Not any numbers on the dial, or to show which way to turn this on!  You just have to turn it all the around hopefully its going to cook Your food right, and its going in the right direction. I let this warm up for 10mins as it said ( although I did it longer)  it took over one hour to grill 2 deboned, deskined chicken breasts, yes they had grill marks! And Almost  2 hours for 3 chicken wings that Never really got crispy, although they where finally done enough to eat safely.  Sitting this on my stove top, it did get some grease on the back of my stainless stove, that I just cleaned that day. So if you put this on your counter, wipe down your counter after using this.  I’m disapointed in this grill, and think it’s senseless when I could have  just use a grill pan on my stove top which works better and faster then this, and the calphalon grill pan is less money.  The Hamilton beach has a non-stick coating on the grill , is easy to clean but takes time,  but not sure if its BPA Free! The bottom part you set the grill into is all plastic. I did put water in the bottom as suggested after I read the Instructions on Amazon. I always put water in the bottom of pyrex when I do a stand up whole chicken in the oven etc. As then Nothing sticks. To use this I had to set this on my stove with the over-head fan going so it would not set off my fire alarms in my house, And I have a open kitchen, with high ceilings.  I think this is pricey for what it is. 2 stars..

Well, it ALMOST lasted a year.  Thank goodness for Amazon's little tracking system that tells you when you bought an item.  I bought this on  May 24, 2014.  It died March 1, 2015.  It was quiet on the two lowest speeds, but loud on the High setting.  I was hoping that the fuse had burned out.  Called Vornado.  They sent a replacement.  It wasn't the fuse.  Thank goodness there's a 5 year warranty on this.  Hopefully I can get a new one for nothing.  I usually love Vornado.  Maybe this is the end of the affair.  I hope not ...  :(
Bought these as Christmas gifts and they were a hit. I added one to our bathroom as well. Works great and easy to clean. I like that these are smaller for small areas like the bathroom.

You get 2 in the set... the rose/pink color is sort of metallic in way...the lids are clear plastic.  The set comes with 2 stainless straws with silicone lip covers and 1 straw scrub brush.  I washed them in my dishwasher - no problem.  The straws have to be washed by hand.  They are the perfect size for moscow mule or other high ball.  I am not a fan of drinking wine with a metal glass - but you could.  Overall, I like them.  Please see my photo.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.
These are cheap bowls and came with a couple scratches on the “copper” coating. They will definitely scratch off more with time. 4 bowls for $20 isn’t bad though. You get what you pay for.

This washcloth is of good quality and actually feels double-ply in thickness.  Since it is white with a stripe it appears very hotel-like in looks.  However, what I don't like about this washcloth is that it gets heavy when wet and the soap seeps into the cloth like a sponge so there is no lather.  I don't consider this washcloth useful for washing for this reason  (it is not a high-pile towel which tends to lather better) and for that I give it 1 star.  No towels ever look new after they are laundered and these are no different; however, they (I received 2) still launder nicely but seem to have shrunk just a wee bit.  I'm going to use these washcloths for other purposes. To me they make good finger towels, trivets for hot plates, and are nice to double up when lifting up a pot lid if I don't have a pot holder handy. Nevertheless, I would not buy them for this reason.  The current price of $9.99 is very steep for this towel even if it were a five-star product.  I hope the price listed is for two washcloths as Amazon does not say this currently in the advertisement. I received two washcloths in the package that arrived for review.  Personally I would not buy these because it makes a good towel but is heavy when wet and prevents soap from lathering.

I would NOT recommend the 12" lid they "suggest" as an additional purchase... it doesn't fit.  But honestly, after thinking about it... I really don't use a lid with this pan.

I am very disappointed in this cordless vacuum because it lacks any “pick-me-up.” I was only able to pick up tiny wisps of cat hair and dust and was barely able to pick up any cat litter sprinkled throughout the carpet or bare floor. If I uncoupled the hand vac from the floor head, then it had a bit more suction but still not enough. However, there is no way I am vacuuming a floor on my hands and knees with a hand vac even if the suction were great. This vacuum is too weak to be useful to me.  FACTS:  - Assembly of the four parts (floor head, hand vac, extension pole, top curved handle) is required.  - The fifth part is the charging cord that plugs into the hand vac portion of the vacuum.  - The floor head has no beater bar (meaning no rotating brush).  - There is no charging light on the vacuum or the charging cord.  - A charge of 16 hours before first use is needed and it is safe to leave on the charger indefinitely.  - The curved handle and pole are not removable once assembled; however, rest of vacuum comes apart for configuring into the various combinations.  - This is a 4-in-1 vacuum meaning there are four vacuum component combinations:   1) with floor head and hand vac attached (short handle)   2) with just the hand vac (meaning no floor head or pole handle)   3) with just the hand vac and long pole handle   4) with floor head and long handle attached to the hand vac – note that this is the typical upright full vacuum.  LIKES:  - Self-standing design with ability to hang from handle hole as well. There is also a wall fitting available for purchase according to the instructions in case one wants to hang the vacuum on the wall.  - Dust bowl and filter are easy to empty and wash.  - Swivel steering.  - "Ease of glide" along my bare floor.  - Two-year limited warranty  - Cordless with lithium ion battery (wall charger cord included)  DISLIKES in order of importance to me:  - There is limited suction, especially when fully assembled as an upright vacuum.  - The vacuum feels cheap like the plastic will break after a while.  - The floor head and hand vac are difficult to couple and uncouple.  - There is no charging light.  - The assembly instructions are not clear when it comes which way to line up the holes in the handle.  In summary, this is truly a bare bones vacuum. To get four stars, it would have to be able to pick up the tiny granules of cat litter sprinkled throughout my floor and carpet. To get five stars it would also have to feel sturdier than it currently feels.

These KEURIG 5000057588 K-CUP COFFEE MAKER RINSE PODS (10 Count, White) only clean your coffee maker’s pod capsule—they are NOT a substitute for regular descaling of your entire coffee maker (see [[ASIN:B00FOT9W5Q Keurig Descaling Solution]]).  The rinse pods cleanse the pod area (where you place the pods) and the channel below, to prevent “flavour carryover” between brews—especially between cups of hot chocolate, apple cider, or fancy flavored coffees and cups of plain coffee or tea.  To use the rinse pods, you need to (1) insert a rinse pod and brew a cup with water; (2) remove the used rinse pod and discard the rinse water; (3) rinse the pod capsule by brewing another cup with water only; and (4) discard the rinse water.  In practice, I needed to rinse the pod capsule twice, because the first rinse produced a cup of hot rinse water with a white ring of chemicals around the water surface.  I think it’s easier to rinse the pod capsule (to reduce flavour carryover) by brewing one cup of plain water and discarding the rinse water.  Doing it that way also avoids adding mildly toxic chemicals to your coffee maker.  The rinse pods contain maltodextrin, sodium bicarbonate, citric acid, and sodium citrate.  The warning on the box says “do not consume”; and, among other things, advises you to “Seek medical attention if a large amount is swallowed, if irritation persists, or if you feel unwell.”

This is a terribly designed Ziplock bag organizer that comes in four separate compartments with one each for quart, gallon, sandwich, and snack bags. Since this is not one unit, it cannot be hung on the wall. There are felt feet  on the underside of the boxes for when it is placed on the counter or in a drawer. However, the beauty of this design is that each compartment can be configured anyway one desires. They can go in several separate drawers or in an L shape in one drawer, or side-by-side, etc.  Unfortunately, the design is terrible. First of all, I can easily load the bags through the large opening but it is designed for the bottom to slide out. This means if I turn over the compartment to load the bags from the bottom, the bags start to fall out of the top. In addition, the bottom is so loosely seated, it slides off if I am not careful when I pick up the compartment. If the bottom does slide off, they also have to be put back a certain way. If I accidentally use the top end and slide it in using the bottom side, it only slides halfway because each end is cut differently.  Personally, this set is a disappointment. No wonder it is so cheap at $17.99. Even though I love the way this keeps all my plastic bags in order, I cannot recommend it. Other options that are available for organizers like this are this [[ASIN:B09D5Y63D1 one]] if one is looking for four separate compartments. For a variety of organizers (separate and one unit), then I recommend this [[ASIN:B09KMNHM2J one]] or this [[ASIN:B09VGP1WRL one]]. For one full unit only, then I recommend this [[ASIN:B09RF759BM rustic organizer]]. For a more modern look, I recommend this [[ASIN:B09WN5PZ6Q one]]. For a larger unit that has room for foil and plastic wrap, then I highly recommend this [[ASIN:B0B1TPD21S one]]. I tried all of these and the style purchased is based on what one desires. I personally like these other ones a little better than this one.

REVISION OF REVIEW IS IN THE UPDATES AT THE BOTTOM  I am really impressed with this soap dispensing sink brush. It fact it is the best I ever tried but I only started using it so time will tell.  WHAT I LIKE  - This is so easy to fill. I just lightly pulled up the plug in the handle to fill it with dish detergent and then pressed down to seal the plug in place.  - One push on the center button and a 5 to 25-cent-sized dollop of soap is dispensed.  - The handle is long and has a nice grip for ease of use.  - The brush bristles are slightly stiff for moderate cleaning.  - The brush head is removable by twisting it to the left to dislodge.  - There's a scraper on the underside of the brush; it's not something I would likely use but it's nice to know it's there if needed.  - The brush comes with a limited lifetime warranty but one has to pay to ship the brush back to them for a replacement.  WHAT I DISLIKE  - I do wish that I could dispense a smaller quantity of soap for the times I'm just cleaning one plate.  - The brush head is not stiff enough for heavy-duty cleaning as the advertising states. I actually like that it's not heavy duty because then the bristles would be too stiff. What I don't like is that they advertise it can be used for that.  - I do not understand why the brush head needs to be replaceable. Perhaps I am wrong, but I seriously doubt the sealing ability of the plug in the handle will outlast the brush head. I would think this whole unit would have to be replaced after a year of use but only time will tell.  If this brush continues to work as it does now, I plan on ordering another one if the price is right. At the time of this review there is no price listed on Amazon.  This brush allows me to clean most of my dishes without even getting my hands fully wet. It's great for moderate cleaning. It's not so great for heavy-duty cleaning or for cleaning small items like baked-on-cheese-covered utensils or greasy items like a butter dish where a sponge covers a better surface area. However, I never expect any dish brush to clean items where rubbing with a sponge would normally be required.  UPDATE Feb 8 - I've been using this brush daily all day long and I decided to lower my rating from 4 to 3 stars. Yesterday was the first time I was unable to dispense the soap. It appears that it might be due to too many soap bubbles in the handle but I can't be sure. After playing around a bit I got it to work. Until yesterday it was working beautifully all day long.  UPDATE Feb 11 - I've decided to downgrade this to 2 stars. Today the soap dispensing ability has mostly stopped working. I had to keep opening and closing it to try to get the soap to squirt out using the air in the handle which they call "air displacement" technology.  Unfortunately, I can no longer recommend this.

I've had this popper for a couple of weeks, wanted to wait until I'd used it to review.  It arrived in quickly (I have Prime) and in good condition with a very well reinforced double cardboard box.  There is a minor assembly to do, the glass front doors need to be attached, the bottom screen tray and the kettle.  It came with a non-slotted aluminum scoop (I traded for one with holes for the kernals to fall through that I already had) a pinch of fairly large popcorn bags (NOT flat bottomed, so you can't fill them and stand them up - I bought some small, flat bottomed bags) some unmarked scoops and measuring spoons. NO sample packets of popcorn were included.  I purchased some 8 oz Great Northern Popcorn premixed bags to try. The directions for use that came with the machine and the on premixed popcorn bags was different and ultimately I used the directions that came with the machine.  After using it, I think either way would have worked fine.  Basically, heat the oil and kettle up first, then add the popcorn and salt works best.  It made plenty for 4 of us to eat full (small) bags of popcorn with a couple of refills. There were a lot of unpopped kernals, but I think I may not have heated the oil long enough. The 8 oz kit was done within about 3 minutes. The popcorn was very tasty... but maybe a little tough. I am going to experiment with my own popcorn and oil to see if this is different.  I estimate for our family gatherings I will need to pop 3 or 4  8 oz popcorn kits. I also plan to try using coconut oil that doesn't have all the yellow dye in it. Further, I purchased a set of 12 different popcorn seasonings for fun. Just sprinkle in the bags after loading them, not in the machine. I was dreading the cleanup, but honestly, it really wasn't bad at all.  I do recommend wiping out after each use to avoid harder to clean build up.  All in all, I am very happy with this suprisingly well made machine. It's glass and metal with some plastic and is fairly heavy.  There is a manufacture date on the base of the machine that says April 2014.  I will quickly update my review if we have any issues.

This is my second Krups (same model) toaster oven. I had the same problems with the first and returned it for the same. Krups has a good name and I just figured it was a fluke. They both lasted very nicely for a couple of years then the buttons got finnicky and would work at all or had to be pushed multiple times to connect. More than once I had no number reading on the temp screen (just lines) and I had to unplug it to reset... No big deal but not what you expect to have to do with an expensive toaster oven. However, my main concern is that the heating elements turn on and never adjusted. They crank up to high and stay there never acheiving the programmed temp. This has happened on more than one occasion. Fortunately, in times before, I had been In the room to catch it. It hadn't happened in a while and unfortunately the other night I forgot about the problem and left the room. I was gone max 2 minutes. When I returned the food inside was completely charred, and smoke was billowing out of the oven filling the house. Unacceptable! This all happened in a matter of minutes and has happened more than once. Both ovens same model have proven to be very dangerous. No wonder it's been discontinued. I have other Krups products that I love. I'm not knocking the company but this was very disturbing and has proven to be a real hazard.

I was excited to try this 13”x9” baking pan as all of the ones I have are glass and often are more difficult to clean and maintain. A metal non-stick pan would be great! Unfortunately, the one I received was damaged. Worse, it is clear it was damaged even before it went into the packing box.  This is a nice, heavy pan that has a good non-stick finish on it. It doesn’t appear to be Teflon which is good. It should work well for things like casseroles, cakes and other recipes that call for this size baking dish.  Right out of the box, my pan was dented up. All four corners have been dented enough to be obvious in both sight and touch. The box in which it shipped was completely fine, though, which tells me that it went into the box already damaged before it was sealed and shipped. The damage was consistent with being dropped at an odd angle and under load, or having been inverted and having heave things stacked on top of it. I do not know if this occurred where it was manufactured or if instead it happened prior to retail shipment. Lack of scratch marks on the finish suggests the former.  I think I can pound it back into shape with a mallet and wooden dowel, but that’s not an acceptable solution. I should be clear, it does not impact usability, but it’s enough to impact the shape of some things baked in the pan.  I really wanted to like this pan but receiving an item that is damaged (and was likely damaged where it was manufactured) is a serious problem. I hope I am the only one who had this issue, but I cannot rate it higher based on a hope or an assumption. It’s a shame; it’s otherwise a nice baking pan.

The purpose of this "industrial-sized" Leifheit cherry pitter is to be able to pit many cherries quickly without having to "reload" a cherry into the pitting chamber.  I figured the need for a lot of pitted cherries when making pies or a large fruit salad would make such a gargantuan unit worthwhile.  I already have the exceptionally designed and almost flawless [[ASIN:B000NQ925K OXO Good Grips Cherry Pitter]] but when this unit came became available, I felt it was worth trying out.  The unit comes with two rubber washers; one is a spare.  What I had to do was remove the cardboard piece holding down the plunger arm and insert the rubber gasket into the slot.  This gasket is needed to prevent the cherry pit from coming back into the cherry after I pulled up on the plunger.  For my test, I tried to pit both the firmer Rainier cherries and the less firm Bing cherries with this cherry pitter. Even though the instructions do not mention pitting olives with this cherry stoner, I decided to try small black Kalamata olives as well--since my other cherry pitter can pit soft olives like this.  I loaded the Rainier cherries into the hopper and they didn't really line up in the pitting chamber; sometimes they had to be pushed into the hole for pitting.  Once I pushed the plunger, I had to pull up fully to release the pitted cherry into the exit shoot.  Of about 50 Rainer cherries I pitted many were damaged on the exit (partially squished or broken) and all but about 4 had the pits remain in the cherry. For the cherries that exited into the waiting bowl, I had to pry open the cherry to remove the pits.  I then tried pitting about 50 sweet cherries and they pitted easier since they were softer cherries.  However, I still had some squished cherries exit the hopper and about 6 of the cherries that exited had pits that had to be pulled out by hand.  Since I also wanted to know if olives would work, I tried about 4 of them and found out quickly this unit cannot pit olives.  As I was washing the unit I noted that the washer was destroyed just from pitting these few cherries.  It appears the plunger not only pitted my cherries but also chopped out part of the washer.  When I inserted the washer, I made sure it was centered and that the pitter blade passed cleanly through the center of the washer's hole.  However, with all the cherry pitting activity it probably moved around and caused large holes in the washer.  Not only does this reduce the effectiveness of the pitting but I had to throw out the washer and put in the spare.  In summary, I cannot recommend this cherry pitter to anyone.  The only redeeming quality it has are the rubber feet on the bottom of the unit that keeps the cherry pitter base firmly in place during pitting.  Other than this, the unit is too huge. The pitting chamber so enormous compared to the hopper you put the cherries in.  Also the plunger cannot be locked down for storage and stands at attention like a rocket about to take off.  The pitter barely pits Bing cherries (the dark colored ones with a firm but softer flesh) and has a 95% failure rate when pitting Rainier cherries.  I had high hopes for this cherry stoner and was sadly disappointed.  I was going to give this unit 1 star but since it did pit the Bing cherries somewhat, I rated this 2 stars.  I have not decided yet whether I'm going to send this to the recycling bin or keep it as a conversation piece, but I doubt I'll ever use this to pit cherries again.

I hate this set of rollers. There are two handles and six rolls. The two handles are installed in two of the rolls and have to be removed once the roll is done to use on the other rolls.  The handles themselves are delicate and I can see the bottom breaking before all the tape is used up.  What I absolutely hate about this set is that it is practically IMPOSSIBLE to remove the label on the roller. I had to remove mine in pieces and had to actually use a scissor to start cutting it off. It took me about 5 minutes to get the label off and it wasted a couple of sheets.  Second, once the label is off, there is nothing to cover the roller for storage.  The Scotch-Brite lint rollers always have a label that can be slapped back on when the roller is not in use. It kept sticking to everything as I have nothing to cover it with. In addition, the handle kept coming apart (see photo). The handle actually has to be seated well within the tube for this not to happen.  I did use the tape and it works very well. It picks up just as good as my favorite brand (Scotch-Brite). I also love that the tape removes on an angle. It is a lot easy to peel off the used tape that way. In this sense, it is actually a better design than my favorite brand.  There are supposedly a total of 120 sheets over 6 rolls, which means there are 20 sheets per roll. This would have gotten 4 stars from me if the label peeled off easily and was used to protect the tape when not in use. As it stands now, I hate this set.

I needed this for an ofc/stuff room that is out in our shop/storage/garage.  The thermometer is critical.  So far, it's run and kept the room dry and comfortable - about 300 sq ft.  It is mostly black plastic, metal grill (dots) in the front.  It does run the fan a few minutes when it shuts down a few minutes to make sure it's not hot.  It would be difficult to knock it over, but it does shut down if knocked over.  Once the power has been disconnected ( power out or it's been knocked over) you will have to reset all settings.  You can use the remote or on the face of the unit using pressure button/controls. The remote is small and flat - uses a coin battery which is included and installed - just remove the clear tab.  There is a flat ledge in the back where the battery can rest - just under the handle grip - which makes it easy to find if you're not really using it a lot.  In our case, we set it and leave it - resetting if we have a power out.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope this review was helpful to you. I really like this dispenser except for the fact that it dispenses just too little soap even on the highest setting. I attached four pictures. The second photo shows the dispenser bottom removed with instruction booklets. The third shows the top battery compartment removed. The fourth shows how much the dispenser actually dispenses.  From left to right is the largest to smallest amount released.  The bottom of this unit holds the soap and the top holds the batteries and controls how much soap will be dispensed. There is an on/off switch under the cap in case one wants to turn the unit off for moving. The instructions are written by someone who has English as a second language and it is not well written, yet they are still understandable.  In order to get the air out of the unit, I dispensed water. It does dispense water generously. However, due to the thickness of any soap, dispensing slows down. I do not want to water down my soap. I got this to release dish detergent and it comes out in a tiny dribble at the highest setting. I would have preferred twice the amount.  In summary, the unit is attractive and large and it does what it is supposed to do when dispensing nothing thicker than water. Nevertheless, when using soap, the dispensing action slows to a mere tiny trickle. Note that this will not dispense into clear glass. In other words, if I want soap to release into a glass container, it will not work. It actually has to see my hand or sponge to release. However, this is to be expected because glass acts like nothing to the sensor but I did not think of this because I often bring up the dish to the soap to dispense.  UPDATE December 6, 2018: I have been using this daily and noticed that it takes about three times to get soap to dispense if it has not been in use for about 30 minutes. This is very annoying. It is as if air gets in the line or the soap goes back down to the bottom of the straw and takes time to come to the top again.  UPDATE December 12, 2018: Although this is operating the same as I described in my update above, I am getting increasingly annoyed with this dispenser. As a result, I have lowered the ranking from 3 stars (a.k.a. 2.5 stars) to 2 stars. Every time that the dispenser has not been in continuous use, I have to try 3 or 4 times to get it to work before it starts dispensing again. At this point I am beyond annoyed.  UPDATE December 22, 2018: I tried so hard to use this soap dispenser and it just dispenses too little soap to be useful. I threw it in the garbage today.

I can see where these could be used in several different ways.  Well worth it.  They are what I would consider a medium thickness or medium duty... not super thin, however, not heavy and thick.  They arrived quickly (i have prime) and in excellent shape along with other things I had ordered.

These are indeed just for mood... they are incredibly thin/sheer.  I just wanted something to soften the wide wooden blinds I have.  Does the trick.  The ivory is a yellowish tint... like ivory would be.  The length and width given are accurate.  Just keep in mind they are "sheers" and therefore really just soften a window.  Also, you will need to iron them out of the package.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful, nor do I shy away from answering comments/questions. Perfect.  I wanted a sturdy, longer spoon to use with my slushie glasses... Seller has contacted me many times offering me Amazon gift cards, starting at $15, now up to $30, to remove my review.  She even claims she will lose her job if I don't.  I won't adjust my review, because it's my word. Pillows are lumpy, but I do use it with another pillow when I need to raise my head.  It was never flat, and does hold my head, just not high enough.  I opened them, and let them sit and rise, one at a time so I could compare how much it's left up. One is actually flatter than the other. It's relatively comfortable, but it's not quite high enough for me, and two of them is too high. Also, if I try to reshape it a bit, it gets all cattywampus.  Overall, I like that they are dense, but not hard like any memory pillow I have tried, I just wish I could get a little more height to it.

So... In an effort to cook with a healthier option to non-stick cookware. This pan is supposed to be seasoned... it did have oil on it when it arrived. It says that if you properly care for the pan it will become more and more "non-stick"... after careful use, cleaning and re-seasoning after each use, it remains pretty awful to cook with. (I've used it daily for about 30 days) The other thing I find is that you will use so much more oil in the process of cooking.... so it's adding a lot more calories to the foods I cook.It may help, as other reviews have said,it would help if the bottom was smooth. I think I am going to go back to the high quality non-stick cook wear and turn the fan on as I am using them at moderate heat. This is my personal experience and it may differ from yours. I will continue to try and use my cast iron pans for high oil content cooking... but I am not going to purchase any more cast iron. My review is an honest reporting of my own personal experience and may differ from yours. I respect that and hope you can be too.

This plastic counter protector replaces one I've had for 25 years and is a much better quality. Although it is designed to protect the counter, I use it as a cutting board as well.  I always place doubled over wax paper on the board before cutting, so as not to cut into the plastic and get plastic bits into my food. Parchment paper would probably be a better choice, so I'll have to try that next time. Very pleased with this purchase. Expensive, but worth the investment.  Update on product: Had to return this counter protector because of very sharp corners It was perfect in all respects except for this.

I purchased this for my daughter who loves all things Nightmare Before Christmas; however, I've ended up returning it prior to gifting. It's quite a bit smaller than I realized. That may be my fault in not checking measurements. It's about the size of a bath towel. In addition, the quality is quite disappointing. I don't like the feel of it. It's almost rough and unpleasant to the touch. The colors are not vibrant the way I'd expected. It's almost like they ran out of color while infusing. Some of the issue might be because I ordered it from the Amazon warehouse which means  items have been returned previously was far as I can tell. They are usually listed as "used." In the case of this blanket, there was a very odd smell to it, almost chemical or sour. But I'm wondering if that is due to someone washing it prior to returning it, and not drying it out well before packing it back up. That's only a guess as I really don't know. But I do know that it's disappointing and I've chosen to return it.

These arrived... all is well... until I loaded them into the dishwasher and noticed that one of the plates had a ceramic flaw - a blue drop that is forever sealed in the glaze.  Now.  It doesn't effect the "use" of the plate... however, the bright blue on white is constantly being picked at to see if some food or other thing stuck on the plate!  The blue of course stands out on the white very brightly.  So... Amazon of course made this right.  But disappointing to have that one plate that I just got rid of because it is so frustrating. The Paula Deen cookware set makes a great, reasonably priced set of cookware for a shower or housewarming present. Although I honestly don't think this set will hold up as well as my Calphalon set (I don't think I would be subjecting these pots to a dishwasher), I believe that for someone starting out (and for less than $100), this set makes a great affordable (and impressive gift). I would be careful using any sharp objects in the cookware as it does scratch easily. However, with using soft spatulas, this set should hold up for years of normal household use. And the name (Paula Deen) makes it a recognizable set to give as an impressive gift.
